member.office_address: 512 Cannon House Office Building Washington DC 20515-2600

form_analysis.form_header_text: Rep. Ryan Zinke MT-01 FY2027 Appropriations Programmatic and Language Request Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Submit this form by March 6, 2026. Please provide all information as appropriate to complete your request. Incomplete forms may result in your request not receiving proper consideration. Thank you for your interest in submitting an appropriations request to the Office of Congressman Ryan Zinke (MT-01) for Fiscal Year (FY) 2027. Below you will find a copy of the submission form for programmatic and language requests for FY2027. Please take a moment to read the instructions carefully. It is important to note that the House Committee on Appropriations will only consider requests for programmatic increases or decreases. In order to provide the information required by the House Appropriations Committee, we have developed the below application form for your use. Please note that you must fill out the entire application. The Committee requires all information for submission. Failing to answer all the questions may jeopardize your request. As noted on the form, please provide us with the President's budget request level (if it is available at the time of submission). If you are submitting multiple requests, you must provide an overall priority ranking for each request. Please fill out separate requests for language and programmatic funding. You do not need to fill out both sections for each request. Please submit this form no later than March 6, 2026. Early submissions are welcome and encouraged. If you have questions, technical issues, or would like to schedule a meeting with a relevant legislative staffer regarding a specific request, please contact our office at (202) 225-5628.

fields.label: All Defense Line Number and Program Element (PE)
fields.help_text: Please tell us the line number in the PBR, Program Element, or program name if requesting language.
fields.rationale: This is a non-defense programmatic request for NASA's Science Mission Directorate. While the field asks for defense line numbers, for NASA Science, referencing the Directorate name is the standard way to identify the account within the CJS bill.

sections.description: Complete this section for funding-specific requests.

fields.label: Please identify the type of request:
fields.help_text: NOTE: Each request requires an individual form, if you have multiple requests please fill out a separate form for each - this includes programmatic requests with language associated.
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic funding request for the NASA Science Mission Directorate topline.

fields.label: Amount/policy included specifically for this item in the President's budget request for FY 2027 (if available):
fields.draft_value: * Currently unknown (President's Budget Request has not been released)
fields.rationale: The FY2027 President's Budget Request amount is currently unknown as noted in the provided funding history context.

fields.label: Description of program request:
fields.help_text: Please give a brief justification for your request and include Agency, Account, Agency Subdivision, Program, Activity, and Dollar Amount.
fields.draft_value:
* Agency: NASA; Account: Science; Subdivision: Science Mission Directorate (SMD)
* Request Amount: $9,000,000,000
* Supports a balanced portfolio of missions in Planetary Science, Earth Science, Astrophysics, Heliophysics, and Biological and Physical Sciences
* Ensures progress on National Academies' Decadal Survey priorities and maintains U.S. leadership in space exploration
fields.rationale: This draft follows the help text requirements using the specified funding ask and program details from the reference values.

fields.label: Funding request for FY 2027:
options: Support the President's FY2024 budget request
fields.rationale: The request seeks a specific funding level of $9 billion for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Specific increase/decrease amount requested:
fields.rationale: This represents the requested increase over the FY2026 enacted level ($7.250 billion) to reach the $9.000 billion funding target for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Federal funding history:
fields.help_text: E.g. $100,000 in FY25, $150,000 in FY26. Please include the complete funding history.
fields.rationale: Provides the authoritative enacted amounts for the two most recent fiscal years as specified in the funding history context.

fields.label: If requesting a funding increase, please suggest an appropriate offset from this bill:
fields.rationale: Suggesting specific offsets is a user-specific policy decision and outside the scope of provided advocacy context.

sections.description: Complete this section for requests involving specific legislative or report language.

fields.label: Description of Language Request:
fields.help_text: Please give a brief justification for your request.
fields.rationale: The request type is specified as 'Programmatic' with 'Report Language Requested: N/A' and 'Bill Language Requested: N/A'.

fields.label: Previous Language Request:
fields.help_text: Please list the fiscal year and provide the exact report or bill language as it appears in most recent House, Senate, or Conference report.
fields.rationale: The request type is specified as 'Programmatic' with 'Report Language Requested: N/A' and 'Bill Language Requested: N/A'.

fields.label: Exact Language Request
fields.help_text: Please include the exact language you are requesting.
fields.draft_value:
- Funding: $9,000,000,000 for NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD).
- Report Language: N/A
- Bill Language: N/A
fields.rationale: The request type is specified as 'Programmatic' with 'Report Language Requested: N/A' and 'Bill Language Requested: N/A'.

sections.description: Information regarding the priority and impact of the request.

fields.label: Are you making this request to other offices? If yes, please list all offices receiving this request:
fields.rationale: This is a user-specific question regarding their individual submission process.

fields.label: Of the requests made by this group in this bill, this request ranks as priority [ ] of [ ] total requests:
fields.rationale: Priority ranking is a user-specific determination based on the submitter's full slate of requests and cannot be provided by the consultant.

fields.label: Why is the request a good use of taxpayer dollars?  Please fully state your justification for use of federal taxpayer dollars:
fields.draft_value:
* Generates a 3:1 economic return on investment, fueling industrial innovation and a high-tech workforce through direct contracts and research grants
* Essential for maintaining U.S. strategic leadership and setting international norms in the face of rapid space science expansion by China
* Funds rigorously peer-reviewed science priorities identified by the National Academies, ensuring nonpartisan and high-impact use of federal funds
* Sustains the specialized domestic talent pipeline and aerospace industrial base necessary for both national security and economic competitiveness
fields.rationale: Utilizes advocacy talking points regarding ROI, geopolitical competition, and workforce stability while referencing the peer-reviewed nature of the missions.

fields.label: Impact on Montana's 1st District and other comments/miscellaneous information:
fields.draft_value:
* NASA Science provided $31.4M in total obligations to MT-01 institutions (FY23-FY25), including S&K Engineering and Research (St. Ignatius) and Montana State University
* S&K Engineering and Research holds a $21.5M contract for specialized engineering support critical to future space missions
* Montana State University (Bozeman) manages $17.5M in grants for high-priority research, including stellar physics and Europa ocean modeling
* The University of Montana (Missoula) received over $4.2M to advance vital permafrost mapping and global climate modeling
fields.rationale: Specifically cites local spending data for MT-1, including the $31.4M total and major recipients like S&K Engineering, MSU, and University of Montana from the provided fact sheet.

top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Delivering specialized engineering support for future human space missions.

top_grants.description: Land- and marine-based ice systems provide Earth analogs for icy moons in general and Europa in particular. Europa contains a liquid water ocean beneath a global ice shell and above a silicate mantle  where coupled geological  glaciological...
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Researching Europa's ocean using Earth's icy environments as models.

top_grants.description: We propose to build and fly a vuv sounding rocket spectrometer  furst: the full-sun ultraviolet rocket spectrograph  to obtain the first high resolution  high quality vuv spectrum of the sun-as-a-star. Our proposed spectrometer covers the 112-200 nm wavelength range with a spectral resolution r>20 000  and will undergo absolute radiometric and wavelength calibration prior to launch. Our team'S extensive combined experience has been brought to bear to produce an elegant and robust payload design that simplifies calibration as well as deployment and operation in Space. A defining goal of heliophysics is to understand the influence of solar radiation  plasma and magnetic field with interplanetary Space   with the planets and smaller solar system bodies. Strikingly  no high resolution spectrum of solar irradiance exists  and construction of such a spectrum from existing data and models is fraught with difficulties. In contrast  Hubble Space Telescope (HST) data of objects including sun-like stars such as alpha cen a are of far higher quality than solar data. Thus we are facing the peculiar fact that we know the inputs of vuv radiation to the heliosphere of a planet surrounding alpha cen a far more precisely than we can for our own heliosphere! Critically  a resolution of>10 000 (<30 KM/S) is needed in the heliosphere  as many objects (planetary satellites  comets  magnetospheres) have comparable orbital and plasma speeds. Uv monitor experiments (including those on ae satellites  sme  solstice  eve on sdo) have r<1200 or so  are quite inadequate for defining accurate irradiances needed for the inputs to many heliospheric problems of interest. As well as advancing heliospheric physics  our experiment will provide a criticalmissing link between the sun and the stars  which will enable unique insights into the heating of the chromosphere and corona. Hst stellar spectra with r>10 000 contain important signatures of magnetic reconnection  accretion  other magnetic processes  and interstellar absorption. But we cannot currently understand how these diagnostic features relate to the spatially resolved data with r>10 000 on the sun. The unknown center-to-limb variations of brightness with wavelengths across line profiles presents a fundamental challenge for which data simply do not exist. Our aim will be to deliver a fully calibrated uv spectrum to the community at the earliest opportunity. This will foster cross-community collaborations across helio- and astro- physics  including some needs for aeronomy of planetary atmospheres including those outside of the solar system. The analysis of furst data will shed light on the fundamental processes of chromospheric and coronal heating  by focusing upon sometimes subtle  yet very significant differences in high quality uv spectra of the sun and the stars. Hst data exist for a dozen or so sun-like stars of a quality already beyond our ability to construct a comparable sun-as-a-star uv spectrum. The new solar spectra will enable us to understand the nature of magnetic energy dissipation as a sun-like star evolves  and the dependence of magnetic activity on stellar mass and metallicity. This proposed research addresses three of the four main goals of heliophysics  as articulated by the decadal panel: 1. Determine the origins of the sun'S activity and predict the variations in the Space environment form nress-300 version 3.0 apr 09 3. Determine the interaction of the sun with the solar system and the interstellar medium. 4. Discover and characterize fundamental processes that occur both within the heliosphere and throughout the universe.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Delivering the first high-resolution solar spectrum for stellar physics insights.

top_grants.description: eo14042 high resolution mapping of surface soil freeze thaw status and active layer thickness for improving the understanding of permafrost dynamics and vulnerability
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Mapping permafrost freeze/thaw status for vulnerability assessment.

top_grants.description: One of the greatest sources of uncertainty for future climate predictions is the strength of climate feedbacks due to respiration  the most uncertain process in the global carbon cycle. Our understanding of global respiration has been limited by obse
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Quantifying respiration for more accurate global climate modeling

top_grants.description: Global analysis for the lisa gravitational wave observatory
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Global data analysis supporting the LISA gravitational wave mission.
